DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Dec-10-2019 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Caroline W. Holmes, Esq., Assistant General Counsel, T:415.538.2324, for respondent/moving party;
Julian Sarkar, Esq. of Sarkar Law, T:415.795.8795, for petitioner/opposing party.

RULING: Court does not allow petitioner to argue. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on RESPONDENT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO PETITIONERS JANE DOES (1) AND (2)'S VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE, INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, AND DECLARATORY RELIEF FOR VIOLATIONS OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC RECORDS ACT, as follows:

Respondent The State Bar of California's demurrer to the sole cause of action for violations of the California Public Records Act in the petition filed Jane Does 1 and 2 is sustained with leave to amend. Petitioners are granted leave to file an amended petition no later than December 31, 2019 using their true names. If petitioners fail to do so, the State Bar will be entitled to judgment in its favor.

Order signed.

In addition, petitioner requested that its motions to compel set for 12/16/19 and 1/15/20 in Department 302 be taken off calendar.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

After hearing, the Court denied PETITIONERS JANE DOE (1), JANE DOE (2)'s Motion To Proceed Anonymously. Order signed.

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Oct-11-2019 Continued To Dec-10-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted on RESPONDENT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION, as follows:

Defendant State Bar's demurrer to the petition for writ of mandate is continued to December 10, 2019. The demurrer raises an unresolved threshold issue: whether plaintiffs may sue using a fictitious name. This is permitted in "extraordinary circumstances," but a prior court order is required. (See Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2019) 6.90.10; Starbucks Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1436, 1452 n.7 ("judicial use of Doe plaintiffs"; "order which maintains the confidentiality of John Doe's identity" (emphasis added; quotation marks deleted).) Plaintiffs have not obtained such an order in this case. Plaintiffs shall file and serve a motion to proceed anonymously to be heard in November.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
